data.title: IAG's Investments Likely to Reap Medium-Term Dividends — Market Talk
data.description: IAG's balance sheet looks "investable" again, with high free cashflow generation despite elevated spending, JPMorgan analysts say in a note. IAG's re-investments, while leading to higher costs in the short term, can potentially drive revenue and higher EBIT margins in the medium term, JPM says. Current EBIT consensus expectations look too conservative, with the potential for earnings upside from stronger pricing driven by leisure demand, JPM says. Shares are up 0.8% at 292.50 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0739 GMT - IAG's balance sheet looks "investable" again, with high free cashflow generation despite elevated spending, JPMorgan analysts say in a note. IAG's re-investments, while leading to higher costs in the short term, can potentially drive revenue and higher EBIT margins in the medium term, JPM says. Current EBIT consensus expectations look too conservative, with the potential for earnings upside from stronger pricing driven by leisure demand, JPM says. Shares are up 0.8% at 292.50 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0734 GMT - Appetite for risk rises after Thursday's trade agreement between the U.S. and U.K. and on optimism about prospects of a U.S.-China trade deal, IG analysts say in a note. Japanese, European and U.S. stock indices climb as investors eye "upcoming U.S.-China trade talks in Geneva with cautious optimism", the analysts say. The pan-European Stoxx Europe 600 index rises 0.4% to 537.8 points while Germany's DAX index gains 0.6% to hit an intraday record high of 23,524.08. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 index climbs 0.4% to 8,563.2 points. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0733 GMT - Oil prices are on track for a weekly gain of more than 3%, buoyed by renewed optimism surrounding global trade negotiations. In early trade, Brent crude and WTI both rise 0.9% to $63.38 and $60.44 a barrel, respectively. The U.S. announced a trade deal with the U.K.--the first with a major trading partner since President Donald Trump imposed blanket tariffs--and is scheduled to meet with China over the weekend. "Easing trade tensions helped lift crude oil prices," analysts at ANZ Research say. "The U.K. deal raises investor confidence that agreements with other countries can be reached despite more complicated issues." Meanwhile, China's exports growth showed unexpected resilience in April month, but the country's oil imports slipped from March levels. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0733 GMT - Puma's start to the year was soft, as expected, amid global trade frictions and macroeconomic challenges, Baader Helvea Equity Research analyst Volker Bosse says in a research note. The German sporting-goods company reported first-quarter sales of 2.08 billion euros, down 1.3% compared with the prior-year period. On a positive note, the group confirmed its expectations for 2025, although it said it can't yet estimate the impact of U.S. tariffs. "Puma needs to gain some growth momentum at the top as well as the bottom line in order to reach its full year guidance," the analyst says. Shares are down 0.6% at 24.45 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0728 GMT - Cellnex Telecom's first-quarter results point to a steady start to 2025, but investors are likely to remain focused on potential asset sales, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. Further disposals should be positive for the Spanish telecommunications-infrastructure company, paving the way for debt reductions and additional shareholder returns, the analysts say. The company said it has already completed 93% of a 800 million-euro buyback program. "A steady start for Cellnex, with profit and cash flows coming broadly in line with expectations and all guidance metrics confirmed," Jefferies says. Shares fall 4.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - EDP made a strong start to 2025, with its first-quarter results exceeding expectations thanks a solid performance in Portugal and Spain, RBC Capital Markets' Fernando Garcia and Charlotte Mettyear say in a research note. The Portuguese utility reported a recurring Ebitda of 1.43 billion euros that topped both consensus expectations of 1.35 billion euros and RBC's estimate of 1.36 billion euros, the analysts say. The company achieved this result despite the fact that it recorded no gains from asset sales last quarter against a gain of 134 million in last year's comparable period, RBC says. The strong results benefited from high hydropower volumes that are likely to continue at least in the second quarter given that Iberian reservoirs are 20% above average as of May, the analysts say. Shares rise 4.7%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - Kakao is unlikely to get a meaningful valuation boost from its projected advertising-revenue increase later this year, HSBC analyst Junhyun Kim writes in a note. The ads growth for the South Korean mobile internet company may come in at 9% on year in 2H, following an estimated 4% increase in 1H, Kim says. Though the company's introduction of feed-type ads and video ads may spur stronger growth, he notes, it is not enough to lead a significant enterprise value upgrade due to a lack of concrete monetization strategies. HSBC downgrades its rating on the stock to hold from buy and cuts its target price by 16% to KRW41,000. Shares slip 0.1% to KRW36,950. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0721 GMT - Campari's results were disappointing, significantly missing consensus expectations, AlphaValue analyst Filippo Ercole Piva says in a research note. The Italian liquor maker booked first-quarter revenue of 665.6 million euros, down 4.2% organically on year, compared to the Visible Alpha consensus forecast of a 1.06% decline. The group attributed the fall to adverse calendar effects and U.S. supply-chain disruptions. The company seems to be facing difficult market conditions, particularly in the U.S., where a softer environment is emerging, the analyst says. Shares are down 3.5% at 5.84 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0717 GMT - Commerzbank's first-quarter earnings beat expectations thanks to a better-than-anticipated top-line performance, RBC Capital Markets' Anke Reingen says in a research note. Net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and pay on deposits--in the first quarter was only slightly down compared with the fourth quarter, RBC says. The German bank's guidance for 2025 net interest income implies an average decline in the quarterly rate relative to the first quarter, the analyst says. Meanwhile, Commerzbank's fees picked up from the fourth quarter, driven by strong growth in its securities business, German private and small-business customers segment, and fees related to capital markets, she adds. Commerzbank shares rise 1.2%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0709 GMT - Travis Perkins's appointment of Gavin Slark, a well respected executive in the sector, removes uncertainty, RBC Capital Markets analyst Andrew Brooke says in a note. Slark is a proven operator in building materials in the U.K. and is well regarded given the value he created during 11 years at Grafton, the analyst says. "We think he is well placed to continue the work TPK has started to refocus and change the way it operates in order to better serve customers and work effectively with suppliers, as well as engage and motivate teams," the analyst adds. Shares are up 4.0% at 606.5 pence. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0658 GMT - Jaguar Land Rover said it welcomes the U.K.-U.S. trade deal as it secures greater certainty for the sector and the communities it supports. "The car industry is vital to the U.K.'s economic prosperity, sustaining 250,000 jobs," Chief Executive Adrian Mardell said in a statement. The British automaker, owned by India's Tata Motors, sells over 400,000 cars annually, with the U.S. accounting for around a quarter of global sales. President Trump and Prime Minister Starmer announced an agreement Thursday that reduces a 25% tariff on all imported cars to 10% for the first 100,000 made in the U.K. There was a blanket 2.5% tariff on U.K. cars exported to the U.S. before President Trump took office. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0656 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher, with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.4% at around 2459. U.S. stock markets continued to rise on Thursday after President Trump presented a new trade agreement with the U.K., SEB analysts say in a note. "The fact that many details of the agreement are still not decided shows how pressured Trump has been to deliver a deal to calm markets," they say. In addition, Trump hinted that tariffs against China may be lowered soon and talks with China will start in Geneva over the weekend. Stock market futures are pointing upwards in both Europe and the U.S. OMXS30 closed at 2449.20, OMXN40 at 2298.23 and OBX at 1433.99. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Germany's DAX Climbs to New Highs — Market Talk
data.description: Germany's DAX rises in early trade and climbs above its previous record closing high reached in March, as the blue-chip index recovers from President Trump's tariff blitz last month. News of the U.S.-U.K. trade deal helps the DAX continue its upward trend, CMC Markets' Jochen Stanzl says in a market comment. "The markets celebrate this agreement not because it shines in every detail but because this rough framework might be sufficient to maintain the temporary suspension of countersanctions permanently," Stanzl says. At home, the government of newly elected chancellor Friedrich Merz is poised to unveil its Agenda 2030 and the prospect of expansive fiscal programs-will drive revenues and profits for DAX-listed companies, he says. The DAX is up 0.7% at 23512.3.(adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Reddit Insider Sold Shares Worth $1,549,335, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Steve Ladd Huffman, 10% Owner, Director, Chief Executive Officer & President, on May 06, 2025, sold 14,000 shares in Reddit for $1,549,335. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Huffman has control over a total of 577,013 Class A common shares of the company, with 577,013 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1713445/000171344525000106/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1746750421.xml

data.title: PRESSR: Visa and Whish Money announce a strategic alliance that is set to revolutionize digital payments and financial services regionally and globally
data.description:
United Arab Emirates: Visa, a global payments technology leader, and Whish Money SAL, a leading regional fintech, are pleased to announce one of the most strategic partnerships in the region. This partnership will enable Whish to leverage Visa's advanced services and technology for payments and money movement, while allowing Visa to expand its service offerings to over 1 million Whish app users. A signing ceremony was held to commemorate this significant collaboration, marking a new chapter in the fintech landscape of the region.

Leila Serhan, Senior Vice President at Visa and Group Country Manager for the North Africa, Levant and Pakistan (NALP) region, commented on the partnership: "This strategic partnership between Visa and Whish is the first in the levant region with an e-wallet and money transfer company, and we align with Whish on multiple pillars, most of which, trust and innovation. This partnership will enable us to bring our advanced payment technologies to a broader audience, facilitating seamless and secure money movement across the world. And as Whish is already present globally and is further expanding its reach, we can further facilitate the international growth through our presence in over 200 countries."

Toufic Koussa, CEO and Co-Founder of Whish Money, added: "We are excited to embark on this new collaboration with Visa which marks a significant milestone for Whish Money. By integrating Visa's cutting-edge technology and services, we are poised to enhance our payment solutions and provide even more secure and efficient financial services to our customers. This partnership underscores our commitment to innovation and excellence in the fintech industry and is a testament to the thorough and careful due diligence Visa undertakes while engaging in such an affiliation given their high compliance standards. Our commitment to compliance and security has enabled us to achieve this unique partnership in the region."

This strategic alliance between Visa and Whish Money is set to revolutionize the fintech sector, bringing unparalleled advancements to digital payments and financial services regionally and globally. As both entities leverage their strengths and innovative technologies, the partnership will not only drive economic growth but also set a new benchmark for excellence and security in the industry.

About Visa:

Visa is a world leader in digital payments, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories. Our mission is to connect the world through the most innovative, convenient, reliable, and secure payments network, enabling individuals, businesses, and economies to thrive. We believe that economies that include everyone everywhere, uplift everyone everywhere and see access as foundational to the future of money movement. 

About Whish Money:

Whish Money is part of Talaco Group that was established in 2004, specializing in technology, telecom, software development, money remittance, digital payments, and logistics industries. Whish has been one of the first global fintech platforms disrupting the distribution of telecom, ISP, gaming, and gift card vouchers, in addition to the digitization of financial services to corporates, retailers, and end users. With over 1,200 agents in Lebanon and 3,000 points of sale in the UAE, Whish Money continues to expand its reach and impact. Today, Whish has offices in Lebanon, UAE, and the USA serving more than 1 million users in over 110 countries.

As a licensed and regulated company by the Central Bank of Lebanon, Whish Money adheres strictly to all local and international laws, regulations, and best practices including stringent Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter-Terrorism Financing (CTF) measures. Whish works closely with local and international authorities to detect and prevent financial crime. Its robust compliance framework, backed by advanced technology and experienced professionals, ensures that every transaction is screened against local and international watch lists, and is scrutinized to identify and mitigate potential risks, enabling it to provide secure and reliable financial services to its customers.

data.title: China launches gold ores blending business in Yantai
data.description:
China, the world's largest gold consumer, has for the first time blended different gold-containing ores in its eastern region of Yantai, state outlet Xinhua said on Friday, as part of efforts to ensure stable supply and lower costs for refiners.

The so-called "bonded gold blending" refers to the physical mixing of gold-containing ores under different customs codes in the pilot free-trade zone in the form of bonded logistics.

On April 27, 279 metric tons of gold concentrates and 28 tons of gold-containing precious metal ores were blended, with the blended products flowing to domestic gold refiners, Qingdao customs said in a statement on April 30.

The blending of gold-containing ores at home will reduce logistics costs by more than 30%, Ma Hongwei, director of the production business department of Yantai port, was cited as saying in the statement.

Previously, the blending of gold ores and concentrates was not allowed in China and imports had to meet required standards, a source familiar with the situation said.

Gold has been under the spotlight with a record-breaking price rally (GOL/) so far this year, helped by growing safe-haven demand amid mounting uncertainties fueled by U.S. President Donald Trump's blanket tariff hikes.

China's central bank has approved foreign exchange purchases by some commercial banks to pay for gold imports under recently increased quotas, Reuters reported this week.

Also, its central bank added gold to its reserves in April for the sixth straight month, official data from the People's Bank of China (PBOC) showed on Wednesday.

Imports of gold-containing ores via Yantai port jumped by 35.1% from the prior year to 158,000 metric tons in the first quarter of this year, accounting for more than 20% of the country's total imports of such cargo.

The "bonded gold blending" is set to increase imports of gold-containing ores in Yantai, where a couple of large-scale gold refiners are located, by at least 5%.

data.title: Xiaomi to Report Solid 1Q Earnings, HSBC Says — Market Talk
data.description: Xiaomi is likely to report another round of solid earnings in 1Q, thanks to increased profitability in the electric-vehicle and Internet-of-Things sectors, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. The bank expects a "beat and raise" set of numbers with net profit hitting 10 billion yuan, more than double from a year earlier, thanks to around 50% on-year revenue growth in its IoT business and robust EV sales, they say. Profit margins are also likely to improve across business segments. In addition, high operating leverage will likely be a key driver for unlocking its earnings potential since Xiaomi is expected to have lower operating expenses in the first half of the year, they add. HSBC Global Research maintains a buy rating on the stock and raises its target price to HK$73.50 from HK$70.40. Shares are last at HK$51.00. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: 17LIVE Group Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
17LIVE Group repurchased 24,700 shares for SG$20,978 on Thursday, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The social entertainment platform has been authorized to buy back around 18.2 million shares. To date, it has bought back 274,700 shares.

data.title: Market Chatter: Takeda Pharmaceutical to Invest $30 Billion in US Over Five Years to Strengthen R&D, Manufacturing
data.description:
Takeda Pharmaceutical will invest $30 billion in the US over the next five years to upgrade manufacturing sites and boost research and development, CEO Christophe Weber said during an earnings call Thursday, Nikkei Asia reported Friday.

Weber said the spending will ensure Takeda's facilities are as efficient as possible and help maintain its US presence, the report said.

Takeda currently runs seven US manufacturing sites, including in Minnesota and California, producing drugs like Entyvio and plasma-derived therapies, the report added.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Mediobanca suitor Monte dei Paschi beats forecasts with 24% profit rise
data.description:
Key points:
  • MPS, Mediobanca both post higher than expected earnings
  • MPS says Banca Generali deal does not undermine its project
  • Surging net fees, lower costs and loan loss charges drive profit

State-backed Monte dei Paschi di Siena on Friday posted a larger-than-forecast 24% quarterly profit rise as it pursues a hostile takeover of rival Mediobanca in the consolidation wave sweeping Italian finance.

Mediobanca, which has announced its own buyout offer for wealth manager Banca Generali in an effort to thwart Monte dei Paschi's (MPS) bid, also reported better than expected results on Friday.

MPS said that work on the Mediobanca bid was progressing and it was not threatened by the planned Banca Generali acquisition.

By contrast, Mediobanca said the risks of combining with MPS were further heightened by the Banca Generali tie-up.

After a deep bad loan cleanup and record profits driven by higher interest rates, Italian banks have turned to dealmaking to support shareholder returns now that rates are falling.

MPS, which Italy has been successfully re-privatising since late 2023 after rescuing it in 2017, is proposing plugging Mediobanca's wealth management and investment banking businesses into its own commercial network, where the two lenders already partner in consumer finance.

Mediobanca, a former powerhouse of Italian capitalism as it started as a lender to large companies, wants to bet instead on wealth management for top tier clients with the Banca Generali acquisition, and continue to steer away from commercial banking.

Mediobanca shares rose fractionally at open, while MPS gained 1.3%. Italy's banking index (.FTITLMS3010) gained 0.5%.

MPS reported net profit of 413 million euros ($464 million), well above a 341 million euro forecast in an analyst consensus gathered by the bank, as revenues held up and costs and loan loss provisions declined.

Revenues were flat year-on-year at 1 billion euros, topping expectations, with higher net fees offseting an 8% decline in income from the gap between lending and deposit rates. Fees from wealth management and advisory in particular shot up 21% on a quarterly basis.

The core capital ratio strengthened further to 19.6% of assets, one of the highest in the sector.

($1 = 0.8908 euros)

data.title: South Korean Stocks Log Fourth Weekly Win
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI edged lower to close at 2,577 points on Friday, as losses in battery makers offset strong gains among auto stocks.

However, the index still rose 0.7% for the week, marking a fourth straight weekly advance, supported by optimism over U.S. trade talks with major economies.

President Trump and UK Prime Minister Starmer unveiled a limited trade deal that keeps the 10% U.S. tariff on British exports but eases duties on cars and expands agricultural access.

Meanwhile, China expressed confidence in managing trade tensions with the U.S. ahead of key tariff talks set for this weekend in Switzerland.

South Korea is also set to hold follow-up talks with the U.S. next week, as both sides work toward finalizing a trade package by July.

Among battery stocks, LG Energy Solutions and Samsung SDI shed 2.9% and 1.1%, respectively.

Additionally, shipbuilders and biopharmaceutical firms faced pressure.

On the upside, automakers Hyundai Motor and Kia Corp both climbed over 1.5%.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 1:05 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 1.% at 79,533, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 1.07% to 24,013, on course for second straight session of losses after Pakistan armed forces launched multiple overnight attacks along India's western border.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 85.5175, as RBI likely intervened to support rupee amid pressure due to intensifying India-Pakistan conflict, traders said.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 102.62 rupees, with the yield up 2 bps at 6.4133%, as traders nervously trimmed positions after Pakistani forces launched multiple attacks along India’s western border.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 2 basis points at 5.64%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) fell nearly 2 basis points to 5.67%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.60%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.90%
